title: Replace acceptable wording in user-facing feedback
description: User flagged liability concern with the term acceptable in feedback labels. Replace user-facing acceptable/outside phrasing with within margin of error / outside margin of error language tied to the Timing Setup setting.
acceptance_criteria: No user-facing app copy or accessible control names use acceptable for feedback; calibration, replay summary, cue descriptions, and preview labels use plain On target / Needs adjustment wording.
close_reason: Implemented accessibility copy cleanup, Timing Setup textbox labels and decimal entry, visual cue parity timing, exemplar playback, stronger cue sounds, and feedback wording updates.

title: Make calibration visual previews pulse and clear
description: Discovered during browser verification: calibration visual preview buttons set the overlay state directly, so preview shapes can remain visible too long instead of pulsing and clearing like the main prototype/shared cue path.
acceptance_criteria: User flash, acceptable cue, and outside cue previews use the shared visual cue timing and clear automatically after a short pulse.
notes: User clarified preview parity: visible circle/shape should be 200 ms like actual feedback, with 500 ms black lead-in and 500 ms black exit matching main:index.html preview behavior.
close_reason: Implemented accessibility copy cleanup, Timing Setup textbox labels and decimal entry, visual cue parity timing, exemplar playback, stronger cue sounds, and feedback wording updates.

title: Preserve Timing Setup values across rapid field edits
description: Discovered during exemplar verification: rapid edits to Timing Setup fields can persist only the most recent field because each update is built from the render's timing object. This can leave exemplars disabled after entering all values.
acceptance_criteria: Editing clear time, full street crossing time, and margin of error in sequence persists all values together and exemplars become available after navigation.
notes: Fixed by keeping Timing Setup draft values in a ref and saving the combined parsed timing state through persistence setters; browser verification showed entering values then navigating to Exemplars enables playback buttons after hydration.
close_reason: Implemented accessibility copy cleanup, Timing Setup textbox labels and decimal entry, visual cue parity timing, exemplar playback, stronger cue sounds, and feedback wording updates.

title: Restore audible loud-street cue sounds
description: Current sound options are too quiet or unpleasant after recent changes. Replace tone patterns and labels with clearer, stronger cues intended to remain audible near a loud street while preserving user choice.
acceptance_criteria: Preview and replay cue patterns are louder and more distinct; labels/descriptions match the sound intent; audio-only and audio-visual modes use the same tone definitions.
notes: User clarified the original sounds were already far too soft near a street; current replacement tones are also unpleasant. Fix should improve audibility and tone quality, not just increase gain.
close_reason: Implemented accessibility copy cleanup, Timing Setup textbox labels and decimal entry, visual cue parity timing, exemplar playback, stronger cue sounds, and feedback wording updates.

title: Fix Timing Setup accessible labels and number entry
description: Timing Setup numeric controls are announced like buttons/unclear values and may announce invalid data during entry. Labels also need user wording: Time to clear from left (first half) and margin of error.
acceptance_criteria: Numeric fields expose explicit editable field names; full street crossing time is announced with the field; incomplete decimal entry does not announce invalid data; current value labels use first half and margin of error wording.
close_reason: Implemented accessibility copy cleanup, Timing Setup textbox labels and decimal entry, visual cue parity timing, exemplar playback, stronger cue sounds, and feedback wording updates.

title: Restore visual cue parity with legacy prototype
description: The rebuilt VisualCuePanel does not match the original prototype visual preview/use behavior. Preview and actual use must share a full-screen cue rendering path matching the original flash, acceptable shape, and outside shape behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Calibration preview, exemplar playback, and practice playback use the same visual cue component; cue visuals match the legacy prototype behavior for user flash, acceptable shape, and outside shape; decorative/idle visuals are not announced unnecessarily.
close_reason: Implemented accessibility copy cleanup, Timing Setup textbox labels and decimal entry, visual cue parity timing, exemplar playback, stronger cue sounds, and feedback wording updates.

title: Fix exemplar playback and labels
description: Exemplars are reported as not working, and mode labels like two markers, two-part halfway, and three markers are unclear. Exemplar controls should play reliably and use Start to Halfway / Start to Finish / Start to Halfway to Finish language.
acceptance_criteria: Each exemplar button starts audible/visual reference playback after a user gesture; labels are understandable without knowing mode IDs; unavailable timing state disables or clearly routes to setup.
close_reason: Implemented accessibility copy cleanup, Timing Setup textbox labels and decimal entry, visual cue parity timing, exemplar playback, stronger cue sounds, and feedback wording updates.

title: Remove internal implementation language from route descriptions
description: User-facing route intro copy currently says things like a screen owns behavior or is intended to be reachable quickly with mobile screen readers. That is implementation guidance, not product copy. Review route descriptions and replace them with concise user-purpose copy or remove unnecessary copy.
acceptance_criteria: No app route description exposes ownership or implementation language; descriptions support the person using the app without explaining code structure.
close_reason: Implemented accessibility copy cleanup, Timing Setup textbox labels and decimal entry, visual cue parity timing, exemplar playback, stronger cue sounds, and feedback wording updates.

title: Fix accessibility and parity regressions
description: Parent epic for the reported accessibility and UX regression cluster across Timing Setup, Calibration/Sounds, Exemplars/Practice, and Visual parity. Child issues track each concrete defect so fixes and follow-up findings remain visible.
acceptance_criteria: All reported child regressions are fixed or explicitly tracked; additional issues discovered during implementation are linked with discovered-from; quality gates pass; changes are committed and pushed.
notes:
User added that exemplars do not work at all; investigate and fix playback/activation in addition to labels.
Read Beads workflow/operations docs and in-beads help. Correct relationship model: original reported work is represented as parent-child beads under epic om-ar9; discovered follow-up should be created separately and linked with bd dep add --type discovered-from. main parity source is main:index.html legacy prototype.

title: Scaffold Next.js TypeScript app shell and route structure
description: Why: the rebuild needs a Vercel-ready App Router foundation before feature logic can be migrated. What: add package and Next.js config, create the TypeScript app shell, route entry files, route-local page hosts, and base global styling according to the Streets and PT Tracker structure rules.
notes: Initial App Router and TypeScript scaffold now exists with layout, thin route entries, route-local page hosts, shared render-only components, global token CSS, public legacy prototype, and package setup for Next 16 plus React 19. Current refinement is mobile accessibility and flow polish before validation.

title: Implement Streets task-first routes and practice behavior
description: Why: the single-file prototype must be replaced with a route-based task-first app. What: implement practice, timing, calibration, exemplars, and preferences routes with accessible navigation, one large primary practice control, replay behavior, exemplar playback, and local settings integration.
notes: Task-first routes are now implemented for practice, timing, calibration, exemplars, and preferences. Current polish focused on phone-first accessibility: no modal timing flow, native controls, route heading focus, no app-driven font sizing, and descriptive sound choice labels plus preview actions for screen readers.

title: Port browser media, announcement, and persistence hooks into Streets
description: Why: practice, cue playback, and accessibility behavior should share focused hook owners instead of route-local ad hoc logic. What: add TypeScript hook foundations for browser audio readiness, browser speech, cue playback, live announcements, route focus, and local persistence hydration and updates for the Streets app.
notes: Practice hooks and browser-service owners are now in place: audio context, speech, cue playback, practice announcements, screen-reader route focus, persistence, and a first practice-session state owner. Refinement is now focused on phone-first accessibility and clearer sound choice UX.

title: Rebuild Streets prototype as Next.js TypeScript app
description: Why: replace the single-file streets prototype with a Next.js App Router rebuild aligned to the approved PT Tracker structure rules. What: scaffold a Vercel-ready TypeScript app, keep the legacy prototype available in parallel, and implement task-first routes for practice, timing, calibration, exemplars, and preferences with local persistence and accessibility-first interaction patterns.
notes: Implemented the first Next.js App Router rebuild pass on branch nextjs: task-first routes for practice, timing, calibration, exemplars, and preferences; shared hooks for audio, speech, persistence, replay, and route focus; shared render-only UI components; public legacy prototype copy; mobile-first accessibility refinements including non-modal timing flow, native controls, no app-driven font sizing, and descriptive sound choice labels with preview actions. Validation passed for Biome, TypeScript, Vitest, and next build.
